page_content:
SOCY450 Investigating Women's Empowerment in Low and Middle Income Countries (3 Credits) Students in this course will learn how the study of women's empowerment in low and middle income countries (LMICs) has developed, its current state of measurement, and new avenues to pursue in the future. Examining the extensive research on gender- based inequalities in educational attainment, employment rates, and health status, students will investigate how power imbalances across individuals, households, and institutional factors result in persistent inequality. Bringing an intersectional perspective to the forefront of the course, we will study how to design effective intervention policies that seek to improve the daily lives of women, girls and their families. Prerequisite: SOCY201 and SOCY202; or permission of BSOS-Sociology department.

SOCY451 Sociology of Culture (3 Credits) Analyzes the relationship between society and culture. How do social forces affect cultural objects and products? How do values and meanings shape individual behavior? How can culture be both a source of domination and resistance? These and other topics will be analyzed to show the role of culture in our lives. Prerequisite: 6 credits in SOCY courses; or permission of the department. Credit Only Granted for: SOCY498C or SOCY451. Formerly: SOCY498C.
